20.reason: [The semicolon after 'else if(n==-1)' makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit immediately even if n is not -1. This logic error prevents processing multiple operations correctly.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The semicolon after 'else if(n==-1)' makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit immediately even if n is not -1. This logic error prevents processing multiple operations correctly.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon after the else if(n==-1) makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit immediately even if n is not -1.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The semicolon after 'else if(n==-1)' makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit after the first iteration regardless of input. This is a logical error that prevents processing more than one operation.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon after the else if(n==-1) makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit immediately after processing any -1, but also after any other operation because break is not inside a block. This results in only one iteration of the loop.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon after the else if(n==-1) makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit immediately after processing any -1, but also causing it to break even when n is not -1 due to misplaced control flow.
